course_description.overview: Mass-participation group ride mirroring Santos Tour Down Under WorldTour stages, starting from Tour Village in Victoria Square/Tarntanyangga, featuring Adelaide's coastal roads, vineyards, and iconic climbs over approximately 150 km[1][2].

key_segments.name: Willunga Hill
key_segments.description: Iconic King of the Mountain climb with steep pitches, featured in pro stages[3]

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Challenging for amateurs with repeated pro-style climbs including Willunga Hill, combined with long distance in summer heat, but supported group format reduces solo suffering[2][5].

logistics.getting_there: Fly into Adelaide Airport (ADL), 15-min drive/taxi to city center Tour Village start[3][4].
logistics.accommodation: Central Adelaide hotels, many at race HQ; book early for January festival[3][4].
logistics.registration_details: Register via tourdownunder.com.au/ride; AusCycling members get discounts; early bird pricing available[1][2][5].
logistics.bike_logistics: Bike hire from Focus Bike Hire at events; fly with bike or rent locally[1].
logistics.local_considerations: Summer heat (30-40°C), hydrate heavily; roads closed for pros, check schedules; family-friendly festival vibe[1][2].

final_verdict.one_liner: Fun, supported ride on pro WorldTour roads with Willunga Hill climax amid Adelaide's cycling festival.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Amateur road cyclists seeking pro experience, social riders, Tour Down Under fans.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for the atmosphere, iconic climbs, and value during Australia's premier cycling week.
pro_tips: Join daily Group Rides for timed segments and peloton support[2]
Train for heat and Willunga repeats[3]
Combine with Epic Ride or festival events[1][5]
Book accommodation early in central Adelaide[4]
